content.problemStatement:
Given a (111-indexed) permutation PPP of the integers in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N], you will first construct an array AAA also of size NNN where Ai=Pi+iA_i = P_i + iAi​=Pi​+i. Now, let f(P)f(P)f(P) as the number of **distinct elements** in the array AAA.

For example, for P=[3,4,1,2]P = [3, 4, 1, 2]P=[3,4,1,2], the constructed array AAA is [4,6,4,6][4, 6, 4, 6][4,6,4,6] and thus f(P)=2f(P) = 2f(P)=2.

Given integers NNN and KKK, find a permutation PPP of the integers in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N] with f(P)=Kf(P) = Kf(P)=K. It can be proven that an answer always exists under the given constraints.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN integers - P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​, a permutation of integers in [1,N][1, N][1,N] satisfying f(P)=Kf(P) = Kf(P)=K.


If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : For P=[1]P = [1]P=[1], A=[2]A = [2]A=[2] which has only one element.
Test Case 2 : For P=[2,1]P = [2, 1]P=[2,1], A=[3,3]A = [3, 3]A=[3,3] which has only one distinct element, i.e. 333.
Test Case 3 : For P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2], A=[2,4]A = [2, 4]A=[2,4] which has 222 distinct elements, i.e. 222 and 444.
Test Case 4 : Explained in problem statement.
